backstory: Freelance full-stack dev, 4 active client repos, charges hourly. Uses Linear for project tracking, Slack for client comms. Privacy-conscious — clients pay for code that doesn't leak.
expected_wow: Two-stage. (1) Four-pillar sweep at turn 2: 'Who's working on it with you' surfaces commit collaborators (3 client teammates) + forward-looking note about Linear team + Slack channel members post-connect; 'What we noticed' surfaces 14 FIXMEs in client code with file/line specificity; 'What we can connect to' surfaces gh + Linear SDK + Slack SDK. (2) Recommend opens post-connect at turn 4 with a `light` move grounded in cross-system data ('PR #43 touches Linear AGY-118 you marked blocked — my read is a PR-to-Linear link-watcher would help you stop dropping these; want me to install or describe something else?'). User likely probes for cross-client privacy isolation (a co-design move surface).
expected_dropoff_risk: Privacy hesitation at scan first-run consent (turn 2). Re-reads §2.0 disclosure carefully. The four-pillar sweep's 'I just reviewed what you've been working on here on your computer. Nothing left your machine.' framing should reassure on the local-only contract. Slack OAuth may still get skipped post-connect.
wow_score_target: ≥ 3 in H5 (signal specificity), H8 (chief-of-staff voice), H10 (recommend proposes grounded), H11 (four pillars render), H12 (primary-CTA isolation — no preview crammed into scan output), H13 (pre/post-connect separation), H15 (recommend opens with light), H17 (any local install for cross-client isolation goes through render-confirm-revoke)
scoring_notes: Privacy-conscious persona — H12/H13/H17 matter most here. Scan output MUST be local-only diagnostic; external data only appears post-connect. The 'after you connect, this expands to your customers + teams' line in the 'Who' pillar is the trust handshake. If recommend's install move emits any local artifact (cron job for cross-client status pings, GH workflow for FIXME triage), the render-confirm-revoke contract must fire — privacy-conscious freelancer needs to see the exact artifact before it lands in their client repo.
